Migraine & Headache.name: Taiyang (EX-HN5)
Migraine & Headache.location: Temples, one finger-width lateral to the outer end of the eyebrow.
Migraine & Headache.how: Use index fingers to press gently in circular motion.
Migraine & Headache.precaution: Avoid pressing too hard, especially if there's inflammation or throbbing.

Migraine & Headache.name: Auricular Helix (Left & Right)
Migraine & Headache.how: Pinch gently between thumb and forefinger.
Migraine & Headache.precaution: Ensure your nails don’t hurt the skin.

Migraine & Headache.name: Auricular Lobule (Left & Right)
Migraine & Headache.how: Massage using thumb and index finger in circular motion.
Migraine & Headache.precaution: Avoid strong pinching or prolonged pressure.

Migraine & Headache.name: Baihui (GV20)
Migraine & Headache.how: Press gently with the middle finger.
Migraine & Headache.precaution: Sit comfortably in a quiet space while pressing.

Migraine & Headache.name: Touwei (ST8)
Migraine & Headache.location: At the corner of the forehead, approx. 4 finger-widths from midline.
Migraine & Headache.how: Press in circular motion using fingertips.
Migraine & Headache.precaution: Avoid pressing if area is tender or bruised.

Temple Pain.name: Taiyang (EX-HN5)
Temple Pain.location: One finger-width lateral to the outer end of the eyebrow (both sides).
Temple Pain.how: Use index or middle fingers to gently press in small circular motion.
Temple Pain.precaution: Avoid pressing too hard, especially during active headache.

Temple Pain.name: Sizhukong (SJ23)
Temple Pain.location: In the hollow at the lateral end of the eyebrow (both sides).
Temple Pain.how: Use fingertips to lightly tap or press and hold.
Temple Pain.precaution: Be gentle to avoid eye discomfort or irritation.

Mid Forehead Pain.name: Yintang (EX-HN3)
Mid Forehead Pain.location: Between the eyebrows, in the center (third eye point).
Mid Forehead Pain.how: Gently press with index or middle finger in slow circular motion.
Mid Forehead Pain.precaution: Use light pressure and a calm setting to avoid tension.

Mid Forehead Pain.name: Shangxing (GV23)
Mid Forehead Pain.location: 1 finger-width into the hairline above the mid-forehead.
Mid Forehead Pain.how: Press with fingertips in a slow circular movement.
Mid Forehead Pain.precaution: Avoid in case of scalp injury or excessive tenderness.

Top of the Head Pain.name: Baihui (GV20)
Top of the Head Pain.location: Top of the head, at the intersection of midline and a line connecting the tops of the ears.
Top of the Head Pain.how: Use middle finger to apply light downward pressure.
Top of the Head Pain.precaution: Sit comfortably and breathe deeply while pressing.

Side Forehead / Hairline Pain.name: Touwei (ST8)
Side Forehead / Hairline Pain.location: At the corner of the forehead, approx. 4 finger-widths lateral from the midline at the hairline (both sides).
Side Forehead / Hairline Pain.how: Use fingertips to press in gentle circular motion.
Side Forehead / Hairline Pain.precaution: Avoid pressing if there's sensitivity or bruising in the area.

Skull Pain / Top of the Head.name: Baihui (GV20)
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.how: Gently press with fingertips in circular motion.
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.precaution: Be seated and relaxed while stimulating this point.

Skull Pain / Top of the Head.name: Shangxing (GV23)
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.location: 1 finger-width into the hairline above the forehead.
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.how: Apply light pressure with fingers in circles.
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.precaution: Not suitable during scalp inflammation or wounds.

Skull Pain / Top of the Head.name: Yintang (GV29)
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.how: Apply soft tapping or circular massage with index finger.
Skull Pain / Top of the Head.precaution: Avoid pressing too long or with sharp nails.

Ear Area Pain.name: Ermen (SJ21)
Ear Area Pain.location: In front of the ear, at the depression formed when the mouth is slightly open.
Ear Area Pain.how: Use fingertips to press and hold gently.
Ear Area Pain.precaution: Do not press with excessive force; avoid in case of ear infections.

Ear Area Pain.name: Tinggong (SI19)
Ear Area Pain.location: In the depression between the tragus and the condyle of the mandible, in front of the ear canal.
Ear Area Pain.how: Press gently while mouth is slightly open.
Ear Area Pain.precaution: Avoid pressing if there is pain while opening mouth.

Ear Area Pain.name: Tinghui (GB2)
Ear Area Pain.location: Just in front of the lower ear canal opening.
Ear Area Pain.how: Massage gently using small circular motions.
Ear Area Pain.precaution: Be gentle and avoid ear irritation.

Ear Area Pain.name: Shangguan (GB3)
Ear Area Pain.location: Above the zygomatic arch, in front of the ear.
Ear Area Pain.how: Press gently in a slow circular motion.
Ear Area Pain.precaution: Use with caution in TMJ or jaw joint disorders.

Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).name: Jiache (ST6)
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).location: On the masseter muscle, one finger-width anterior and superior to the angle of the jaw (both sides).
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).how: Clench teeth to locate muscle, then massage slowly with fingertips.
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).precaution: Avoid excessive pressure on the jaw joint.

Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).name: Xiaguan (ST7)
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).location: In front of the ear and below the zygomatic arch (cheekbone).
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).how: Apply firm circular massage with fingertip.
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).precaution: Avoid pressing while chewing or talking.

Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).name: Quanliao (SI18)
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).location: Directly below the outer corner of the eye, in the hollow of the cheekbone.
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).how: Press gently using index fingers in small circles.
Jaw and Cheek Pain (TMJ).precaution: Avoid excessive pressure near the eye socket.

Back of the Head/Neck Pain.name: Waiguan (SJ5)
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.location: Two finger-widths above the wrist crease, between the two tendons on the back of the forearm.
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.how: Apply firm pressure with thumb or knuckle.
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.precaution: Avoid if there’s skin damage or nerve sensitivity.

Back of the Head/Neck Pain.name: Fengchi (GB20)
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.location: Below the base of the skull, in the depressions on either side of the neck muscles.
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.how: Massage with thumbs in upward circular motion.
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.precaution: Use light pressure if dizzy or sensitive.

Back of the Head/Neck Pain.name: Auricular Lobule
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.how: Massage gently between thumb and index finger.

Back of the Head/Neck Pain.name: Yintang (GV29)
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.how: Press gently in a circular motion.
Back of the Head/Neck Pain.precaution: Be gentle to prevent eye strain.
